tape: S&P +0.2%/Nasdaq +0.5% but Dow -0.3% as cooler June CPI (3.5% vs 3.8% est) offsets oil at $80.05 (+$1.86) on the Strait of Hormuz 20% cargo-toll blockade; VIX down to 15.35 (-3%) and quant_regime.json's KMeans cluster reads calm_drift (vol 14.1% ann) though the HMM side still reads trending -- geopolitical event risk (Hormuz toll, big-bank earnings today) isn't fully captured by either.

alerts.headline: Data-quality flag, not news: prior session materials cited NTSK at a 'fresh 52-week high of $21.69,' but current search confirms the actual 52-wk high is $27.99 and the stock is trading $12.41-13.69 today (matches broker's $13.63) -- well below that cited high. Worth a human check on which price/field got mixed up before it affects confidence calibration on this held position.

Cross-check disagreement: FinBERT reads ABSI mean sentiment -0.47 (fairly negative, n=2 headlines) today even as the desk's own watchlist thesis is bullish (analyst PT hikes, Phase 1 data). No fresh negative catalyst found in search -- Morgan Stanley/H.C. Wainwright PT hikes confirmed real (to $10/$16) and stock is +1.94% today -- so this looks like FinBERT keying off residual dilution-offering language rather than new bad news, but flagging the divergence per protocol.
Susquehanna trimmed PT to $7 from $8 (still Positive rating) alongside the ongoing $1B+ Equinor backlog story; no new catalyst since Chad Deaton's 7/2 insider buy.

No new news since CEO Bhappu's $968k (7/7) and chairman Hansen's (7/8) insider buys. The $725M OSC financing (6/18) and Australian Strategic Materials acquisition (announced 1/20) are known, already-priced catalysts -- one source notes the ASM deal completion has slipped to August, a delay not a cancellation.
No new news; Caris Detect commercial launch, $100M buyback, and NYSE Texas dual listing remain the latest known items.
No fresh news; $140M-cap buyback and an already-dated analyst PT trim (~$8.88) are the only recent items, nothing from the last few hours.

No new news; DLA bridge-mod contract extension to 11/29/26 remains the latest catalyst. The Solstice Advanced Materials HFO licensing deal that turned up in search is old (3/27/26), not fresh. Earnings 7/29.

Not held/not on watchlist, but the bot's critic vetoed a WRAP buy three separate times in the last ~20 min (dilution-history/pump-signature failures); stock is down -4.2% today, giving back most of Thursday's 48% pop, and no fresh catalyst found beyond a generic cash-burn/dilution risk note and an older ATF BolaWrap regulatory item. Consistent with today's critic vetoes -- not a buy.
